title: A Treatise on Political Economy
description: A Treatise on Political Economy — knowledge fragments for AI agents

memes.label: About This Wiki
memes.description: A knowledge wiki for "政治经济学概论" — this page is auto-generated from book metadata

memes.description: 'Table of contents for A Treatise on Political Economy, Volume I, Book I: Of the Production of Wealth'

memes.label: Adam Smith
memes.description: 'Scottish economist and philosopher, author of The Wealth of Nations (1776), founder of classical political economy'

memes.label: Advances of Capital
memes.description: 'The sums of capital laid out by the entrepreneur in advance of production returns — covering wages, raw materials, and tools — treated by Say as the fundamental mechanism by which industry is set in motion.'

memes.label: Adventurer
memes.description: "Say's term for the entrepreneur or risk-bearing capitalist who organizes productive industry, distinct from the mere capitalist and the labourer."

memes.label: 'Advertisement'
memes.description: 'Translator''s advertisement by C.R. Prinsep, 1821'

memes.label: Agriculture
memes.description: 'The cultivation of the soil for the production of food and raw materials, regarded by the Physiocrats as the sole productive sector (002-006)'

memes.label: Apprentice
memes.description: 'A system of craft training in which novices are bound to masters for a fixed term to learn a trade, analyzed by Say as a regulatory barrier to labour mobility and industrial efficiency.'

memes.label: 'Balance of Trade'
memes.description: 'The accounting of the value of a nations exports against its imports; a mercantilist concept that Say systematically critiques as an illusory measure of national wealth.'

memes.label: Bank-note
memes.description: 'A promissory note issued by a bank, payable on demand in specie, serving as a paper substitute for metallic currency in commercial circulation.'

memes.label: 'Bank of England'
memes.description: 'The central bank of the United Kingdom, established in 1694, whose paper money and suspension of specie payments are extensively analyzed in Say''s Treatise on Political Economy.'

memes.label: Banking
memes.description: 'The business of receiving deposits, making payments on behalf of others, and providing credit instruments such as bills of exchange (009-001)'

memes.label: Bankruptcy
memes.description: 'The failure to meet financial obligations, analysed by Say as both a private commercial failure and a state-level monetary phenomenon effected through coinage debasement and paper-money depreciation.'

memes.label: Barter
memes.description: 'Direct exchange of goods for goods without the intermediation of money, treated by Say as the primitive form of commerce that necessarily gives way to monetary exchange as the division of labour advances.'

memes.label: Bill of Exchange
memes.description: "A negotiable financial instrument used to transfer funds between distant parties; discussed by Say in the context of money substitutes and international trade."

memes.label: Export Bounty
memes.description: 'A government payment or subsidy granted to exporters, treated by Say as a mercantilist distortion that transfers wealth from taxpayers to foreign consumers while misdirecting domestic industry.'

memes.label: Bullion
memes.description: 'Gold or silver in bar or ingot form, valued by weight and fineness rather than by coinage stamp; the raw material of metallic currency and a key object of monetary policy in classical economics.'

memes.label: Capital
memes.description: 'Accumulated values devoted to productive employment, distinct from revenue used for immediate consumption (003-002)'

memes.label: Capitalist
memes.description: 'An economic agent who furnishes the funds for productive enterprise; the owner of accumulated capital who advances it to production in return for a share of the product.'

memes.description: 'Defining production and the nature of wealth'

memes.label: 'Chapter X: Of the transformations of capital in production'
memes.description: 'How capital is transformed, perpetuated, and increased through production'

memes.label: 'Chapter XI: Of the formation and multiplication of capital'
memes.description: 'The processes by which capital is formed and multiplied'

memes.label: 'Chapter XII: Of unproductive capital'
memes.description: 'Capital withdrawn from both consumption and productive employment'

memes.label: 'Chapter XIII: Of immaterial products'
memes.description: 'Values consumed at the moment of production — services, utilities, and pleasures'

memes.label: 'Chapter XIV: Of the right of property'
memes.description: 'Property rights as the fundamental encouragement to the multiplication of wealth'

memes.description: 'Say''s Law: the theory of markets, demand, and the vent for products'

memes.label: 'Chapter XVI: Of the benefits of brisk circulation of money and commodities'
memes.description: 'The effects of rapid circulation on production and wealth'

memes.label: 'Chapter XVII: Of government regulations intended to influence production'
memes.description: 'Government interventions: product regulation, trade balance, privileged companies, corn trade'

memes.label: 'Chapter XVIII: Of the productive efforts of public authority'
memes.description: 'When government enterprises succeed or fail at creating national wealth'

memes.description: 'The economic effects of colonies on the mother country'

memes.description: 'Agricultural, manufacturing, and commercial industry and their concurrence in production'

memes.label: 'Chapter XX: Of temporary and permanent emigration'
memes.description: 'The effects of emigration on national wealth'

memes.label: 'Chapter XXI: Of the nature and uses of money'
memes.description: 'A comprehensive analysis of money: coinage, standard, value, and measurement'

memes.label: 'Chapter XXII: Of signs or representatives of money'
memes.description: 'Bills of exchange, banks of deposit, bank-notes, and paper-money'

memes.label: 'Chapter III: Of the nature of productive capital'
memes.description: 'The tools, subsistence, and raw materials that constitute productive capital'

memes.label: 'Chapter IV: Of natural agents that assist in production'
memes.description: 'Land and other natural agents as sources of productive value'

memes.label: 'Chapter V: How industry, capital, and natural agents unite for production'
memes.description: 'The union of productive sources: wages, interest, and rent'

memes.label: 'Chapter VI: Of the operations common to all branches of industry'
memes.description: 'The three operations: theoretical knowledge, application, and manual execution'

memes.label: 'Chapter VII: Of the labour of mankind, of nature, and of machinery'
memes.description: 'Productive labour, natural agency, and machinery compared'

memes.label: 'Chapter VIII: Of the advantages and disadvantages of division of labour'
memes.description: 'The extent and consequences of the division of labour'

memes.label: 'Chapter IX: Of the different methods of employing commercial industry'
memes.description: 'Commercial industry methods and their role in production'

memes.label: Circulation
memes.description: 'The continual interchange of commodities and money that facilitates production and consumption; its briskness is essential to national prosperity (016-001)'

memes.label: Coin
memes.description: 'Metallic money stamped by public authority to certify its weight and fineness; a medium of exchange whose value derives from both its metallic content and the government guarantee.'

memes.label: Coinage
memes.description: 'The process of stamping metal into certified coin, treated by Say as a public function that adds value to bullion through authentication and standardization.'

memes.label: Colony
memes.description: 'A settlement established by a mother-country, subject to exclusive commercial relations that restrict its trade to the founding nation (019-011)'

memes.label: Commerce
memes.description: "The activity of buying, selling, and exchanging goods, encompassing both internal (domestic) and external (international) trade, which Say analyzes as a branch of industry that creates value through the transport and distribution of products."

memes.label: Commercial Industry
memes.description: "The branch of industry that brings products within reach of consumers by transporting and distributing goods."

memes.label: Commodity
memes.description: 'A product or article of commerce that possesses exchangeable value; the object of trade and the medium through which value is realized in the market.'

memes.label: Competition
memes.description: 'The rivalry among producers and traders that tends to reduce prices and profits to their natural level, a central mechanism of classical economics (002-005)'

memes.label: Consumption
memes.description: 'The destruction of value through use, which may be productive (reinvested) or unproductive (final)'

memes.label: 'Contents'
memes.description: 'Table of contents for Volume I, Book I: Of the Production of Wealth'

memes.label: Trade Corporation
memes.description: 'A chartered association of tradesmen or craftsmen with exclusive privileges, treated by Say as a restraint on industry that protects entrenched interests at the expense of competition, innovation, and labour mobility.'

memes.label: Credit
memes.description: 'A financial instrument allowing deferred payment, enabling commerce to proceed without immediate specie settlement (009-001)'

memes.label: David Ricardo
memes.description: 'British political economist, developed the labour theory of value and the theory of comparative advantage'

memes.label: Debt
memes.description: 'Financial obligation incurred by borrowing, treated by Say in its public and private dimensions — national debt as a burden on productive capital, and the debtor-creditor relationship as central to monetary integrity.'

memes.label: Demand
memes.description: 'The desire and ability to purchase goods, which Say argued is created by production itself rather than by money or consumption.'

memes.label: Depreciation
memes.description: "The decline in the exchangeable value of money or assets relative to other commodities; a key concept in Say's analysis of paper currency and monetary stability."

memes.label: Division of Labour
memes.description: 'The separation and specialization of productive operations among different workers and classes'

memes.label: Education
memes.description: 'The cultivation of knowledge, skill, and industrious habits through instruction; regarded by Say as a form of immaterial investment that enhances the productive powers of the population.'

memes.label: Emigration
memes.description: 'The movement of people, industry, and capital from one nation to another; analyzed by Say in terms of its economic causes and effects on national wealth in Chapter XX of the Treatise.'

memes.label: Enterprise
memes.description: 'A business undertaking or commercial venture that combines capital, labour, and natural agents under entrepreneurial direction to produce goods or services for market exchange.'

memes.label: Exchange
memes.description: 'The mutual transfer of values between parties, each giving what they value less for what they value more'

memes.label: 'Free Trade'
memes.description: 'The policy of unrestricted international commerce, free from tariffs, prohibitions, and government interference; advocated by Say as the natural condition most conducive to the prosperity of all nations.'

memes.label: Frugality
memes.description: 'The classical economic virtue of restrained consumption and accumulation of savings, treated by Say as a primary driver of capital formation and national prosperity.'

memes.label: Government Regulations
memes.description: 'State actions intended to influence production, which Say argues generally diminish rather than increase national wealth'

memes.label: Henry IV of France
memes.description: 'King of France (1589–1610), whose reign of economic reconstruction under the ministry of Sully is cited by Say as an example of the productive potential of enlightened economic policy'

memes.label: Hoard
memes.description: 'The unproductive accumulation of money or precious metals in idle storage — condemned by Say as a withdrawal of capital from productive circulation.'

memes.label: Home Market
memes.description: "The domestic market for the products of a nation, distinguished from foreign markets in the analysis of trade policy, value measurement, and the territorial division of labour advanced by Say."

memes.label: Immaterial Products
memes.description: 'Values consumed at the moment of their production, such as a physician''s advice or a musician''s performance'

memes.label: Industry
memes.description: 'Human agency applied to production, comprising scientific knowledge, entrepreneurial application, and manual execution'

memes.label: Insurance
memes.description: "A commercial arrangement by which the risk of loss is distributed among many, each paying a small premium to protect against a large contingent loss; in Say's Treatise, insurance is a productive service that reduces uncertainty and enables long-distance trade."

memes.label: 'Interest of Money'
memes.description: 'The price paid for the loan of capital, a key variable in classical political economy reflecting the abundance or scarcity of capital'

memes.label: Intrinsic Value
memes.description: 'The inherent worth of a commodity derived from its utility and cost of production, distinguished by Say from relative or exchange value as a property of the thing itself rather than a ratio of exchange.'

memes.label: Invention
memes.description: 'The creation or discovery of new machines, processes, and techniques that enhance the productive powers of labour; a primary driver of economic progress when protected by adequate intellectual property rights.'

memes.label: 'Sir James Steuart'
memes.description: 'English political economist, author of An Inquiry into the Principles of Political Economy (1767), a major precursor to Adam Smith'

memes.label: Jean-Baptiste Colbert
memes.description: 'French statesman and finance minister under Louis XIV, associated with mercantilist economic policy'

memes.label: Labour
memes.description: 'The human agency applied to production — the action of man on nature to create utility and value (001-007)'

memes.label: Labouring Class
memes.description: "The social class of wage-earning manual workers whose economic condition — employment, subsistence, and the effects of machinery and the division of labour — Say analyzes within the framework of classical political economy."

memes.label: Loan
memes.description: 'The transfer of capital from a lender to a borrower for productive employment, a key mechanism of capital mobilization in classical political economy'

memes.label: Louis XIV
memes.description: "King of France (1643–1715), whose prodigal spending, wars, and mercantilist policies serve as Say's primary historical example of the economic damage caused by government extravagance and misdirected public expenditure"

memes.label: Machinery
memes.description: 'Tools and mechanical contrivances that augment human productive power; Say defended machinery against the charge that it destroys employment.'

memes.label: Manufacture
memes.description: "The branch of industry that transforms raw materials into finished products through the application of labour and machinery, creating what Say terms form-utility — value added to matter by changing its shape, composition, or arrangement."

memes.label: Manufacturing
memes.description: 'The branch of industry that transforms raw materials into finished goods through mechanical or chemical processes (002-001)'

memes.label: Market
memes.description: 'The institutional framework — both physical and abstract — through which buyers and sellers exchange products, services, and value, forming the central coordinating mechanism of commercial society.'

memes.label: Merchant
memes.description: 'An economic agent engaged in the purchase and resale of goods; the intermediary who connects producers and consumers across markets through the mechanism of commerce.'

memes.label: Money
memes.description: 'A commodity whose value serves as a common measure and medium of exchange'

memes.label: Monopoly
memes.description: 'An exclusive right or privilege that restricts competition, enabling the holder to charge prices above the natural rate (017-035)'

memes.label: Montesquieu
memes.description: 'French political philosopher (1689–1755), author of The Spirit of the Laws, cited by Say on the relationship between money, prices, commerce, and legal institutions'

memes.label: Mortgage
memes.description: 'A loan secured by land, an instrument that converts fixed landed property into circulating capital in classical political economy'

memes.label: Napoleon
memes.description: 'Emperor of the French (1804–1814, 1815), whose Continental System of economic warfare and blockade policy is critiqued by Say as economically self-defeating, impoverishing France while failing to achieve its strategic objectives'

memes.label: National Capital
memes.description: 'The aggregate capital stock of a nation, comprising all reproductive resources employed in production, as distinguished from national revenue and national wealth.'

memes.label: National Wealth
memes.description: 'The aggregate of all values possessed by the members of a nation; the sum total of individual and public property, distinct from the mere accumulation of money or precious metals.'

memes.label: Natural Agents
memes.description: 'The gratuitous assistance of nature in production, including land, water, sunlight, and physical forces'

memes.label: 'Necessaries'
memes.description: 'The basic goods and services required to sustain life and maintain a labouring population, forming the lower bound of consumption in classical political economy.'

memes.label: Paper-Money
memes.description: 'Fiat paper currency issued without specie convertibility, which Say analyses as a distinct monetary form whose value derives from limitation of quantity rather than intrinsic worth.'

memes.label: Patent of Invention
memes.description: 'An exclusive legal privilege granted to an inventor for a limited term, treated by Say as a justified temporary monopoly that rewards innovation while acknowledging its cost to consumers.'

memes.label: Plantation
memes.description: "A large-scale agricultural enterprise in colonial territories, typically producing cash crops for export, analyzed by Say in the context of colonial economics, slave labour productivity, and the relationship between metropolitan and colonial production."

memes.label: 'Political Economy'
memes.description: 'The science of the production, distribution, and consumption of wealth, as defined and developed in Say''s Treatise.'

memes.label: Population
memes.description: "The total body of inhabitants within a territory; in Say's Treatise, population is both a productive resource and a constraint — its density, industry, and relation to subsistence determine economic prosperity."

memes.label: Precious Metals
memes.description: 'Gold and silver, used both as the material of money and as commodities in manufacture; their value, like that of all other goods, is governed by the law of supply and demand.'

memes.label: Price
memes.description: 'The quantity of money readily parted with to obtain a thing; the monetary measure of exchangeable value'

memes.label: Production
memes.description: 'The creation of utility — not of matter, but of value — through the combined agency of industry, capital, and natural agents.'

memes.label: Productive Capital
memes.description: 'Pre-existing products employed as means of further production'

memes.label: Productive Consumption
memes.description: "The distinction drawn by Say between consumption that reproduces value (productive/reproductive) and consumption that destroys value without return (unproductive), a core mechanism of capital formation theory."

memes.label: Productive Power
memes.description: 'The capacity of labour, capital, and natural agents to generate output, which Say identifies as the fundamental engine of national wealth — distinct from the mere accumulation of capital or extension of territory.'

memes.label: Profit
memes.description: 'The return to the entrepreneur or capitalist for undertaking productive activity, after deducting costs of production (002-006)'

memes.label: Prohibition
memes.description: 'The government ban or prohibitive duty on the import of foreign goods, treated by Say as an extreme and counterproductive form of trade restriction that harms both consumer and producer alike.'

memes.label: Property
memes.description: "The exclusive right of an individual or entity to possess, use, and dispose of a thing; in Say's Treatise, property is the foundational institution of economic activity, without which production, capital accumulation, and exchange cannot flourish."

memes.label: Protectionism
memes.description: 'The policy of shielding domestic industries from foreign competition through tariffs, prohibitions, and other government restrictions; systematically critiqued by Say as economically self-defeating and a source of artificial scarcity.'

memes.label: Public Authority
memes.description: "The role of government in economic life as analyzed by Say: a necessary institution for security and public works, but prone to harmful interference."

memes.label: 'Raw Material'
memes.description: 'The unprocessed or partially processed physical inputs used in manufacturing, distinct from tools, implements, and labour in Say''s analysis of productive capital.'

memes.label: Relative Value
memes.description: 'The exchange ratio between two commodities, distinguished by Say from intrinsic value as a relational rather than inherent property, determined by the comparative costs of production and market conditions of supply and demand.'

memes.label: Rent
memes.description: "The annual return yielded by land and other natural agents; discussed by Say in the context of productive capital and the right of property."

memes.label: Revenue
memes.description: 'The annual income or produce accruing to individuals or the nation, distinct from capital which is reserved for productive employment (012-002)'

memes.label: Right of Property
memes.description: 'The legal and social recognition of exclusive ownership, essential for production and capital accumulation'

memes.label: Saving
memes.description: 'The act of setting aside part of current income for future productive use, the foundation of capital accumulation (002-006)'

memes.label: Scarcity
memes.description: 'The economic condition in which resources, goods, or money are insufficient relative to demand, influencing prices, production, and the distribution of wealth.'

memes.label: Seignorage
memes.description: 'The charge or duty levied by the sovereign on the coinage of precious metals — treated by Say as a legitimate public revenue source when moderate, but a disguised tax when excessive.'

memes.label: 'J.C.L. Sismondi'
memes.description: 'Swiss historian and economist, critic of classical political economy and early advocate of social intervention'

memes.label: Smuggling
memes.description: "The illegal importation or exportation of goods in violation of trade restrictions and revenue laws; in Say's Treatise, smuggling is analyzed not as a moral failing but as a predictable economic response to excessive duties and trade prohibitions."

memes.label: Speculation
memes.description: 'A mode of commercial activity in which goods are purchased with the intent of reselling them at a higher price, capitalizing on price fluctuations rather than adding value through production or transformation.'

memes.label: Stock
memes.description: "Accumulated goods — tools, materials, livestock, merchandise — that serve as the physical basis of productive capital in Say's framework."

memes.label: Subsistence
memes.description: "The minimum of food, shelter, and necessities required to sustain human life; a central concept in classical political economy where the ratio of population to subsistence determines wages and living standards."

memes.label: Supply
memes.description: 'The quantity of goods, services, or commodities brought to market by producers; one of the two fundamental forces — with demand — that determine prices and direct the allocation of productive resources.'

memes.label: Surplus
memes.description: 'The excess of production over consumption; the source of saving, capital accumulation, and economic growth in classical political economy.'

memes.label: Tax
memes.description: 'A compulsory levy imposed by government on individuals or products, forming the principal source of public revenue (001-005)'

memes.label: Taxation
memes.description: "The system and policy by which government raises revenue through compulsory levies on individuals and property, analyzed by Say in terms of its economic incidence, excess burden, and effects on capital accumulation and productive activity."

memes.label: Thomas Malthus
memes.description: 'English economist and demographer, author of An Essay on the Principle of Population'

memes.label: 'Title Page'
memes.description: 'Title page of A Treatise on Political Economy, Volume I, 1821'

memes.label: Transport
memes.description: 'The movement of goods from one place to another, which Say identifies as a form of production that adds place-utility to commodities.'

memes.label: 'Anne Robert Jacques Turgot'
memes.description: 'French economist, statesman, and Physiocrat, known for his early contributions to political economy, notably on capital formation and commerce'

memes.label: Unproductive Capital
memes.description: 'Capital that is consumed without reproduction — spent on goods, services, or display that satisfy immediate wants but yield no fresh product, in contrast to productive capital which generates new value.'

memes.label: Utility
memes.description: 'The inherent fitness or capability of certain things to satisfy the various wants of mankind'

memes.label: Value
memes.description: 'The quality arising from the combination of utility and difficulty of attainment'

memes.label: 'Vent'
memes.description: 'The principle that supply creates its own demand — products are paid for with products'

memes.label: Voltaire
memes.description: 'French Enlightenment writer and philosopher (1694–1778), cited by Say as an astute observer of economic phenomena including trade, money, and the effects of government policy on national prosperity'

memes.label: Wages
memes.description: 'The remuneration paid to labourers for their productive services, forming one of the costs of production (002-012)'

memes.label: Wealth
memes.description: 'An indefinite quantity of objects possessing real and intrinsic value'
